should_trigger: Design a multi-agent orchestrator that takes vague business goals, decomposes them into subagent work, tracks evidence, verifies outputs, and reports whether the original stakeholder need was satisfied.
Our agent pipeline keeps passing unit checks but users say the final answer misses the real task. Build a workflow that separates verification from validation and traces requirements to scenarios.
We need to define interfaces between planner, executor, browser tool, memory store, verifier, and human approval so long-running tasks do not lose state or violate policy.
Help me compare two architectures for an autonomous coding agent under budget, safety, and latency constraints, including risks and lifecycle gates.
should_not_trigger: Summarize what systems engineering means in one paragraph for a glossary.
Pick the best CLI command to list files in this directory.
Write a simple todo list for cleaning my desk.
edge_cases: I have a short two-step automation, but it touches production data and human approval. Decide whether a lightweight systems engineering pass is warranted and tailor the workflow if so.

compliance_expectations.runtime_dependency: Must answer from SKILL.md content only; do not require external source files, webpages, source reports, original books, external source folder, or distilled source text.
compliance_expectations.activation_gate: Should trigger only when coupling, risk, lifecycle span, stakeholder ambiguity, traceability, interfaces, or V&V needs justify systems-engineering formality.
compliance_expectations.expected_behavior: Identify system-of-interest, mission need, lifecycle phase, and stakeholder expectations, then produce operational scenarios, requirements trace, functional decomposition, interfaces, tradeoffs/risks, V&V, and transition criteria.
compliance_expectations.negative_behavior: Must not equate subtask completion with validation, allocate work before functions/interfaces are clear, or hide requirement/configuration changes.
